---
title: "ANCOMBC-II Differential Abundance Analysis"
author: "Nitin Bayal"
format: html
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Differential Abundance Analysis

This document presents the Differential Abundance Analysis (DAA) using the ANCOMBC-II method. The analysis identifies significant features between diet groups and visualizes the top findings.

### Data

```{r data}
# Load functions
source("funct.R")

# Load TreeSummarizedExperiment object
tse <- readRDS("../data/tse.Rds")

# Preprocess data
# Agglomerate at genus level and filter by prevalence
tse <- agglomerateByPrevalence(
  tse, rank = "genus",
  prevalence = 0.1,
  assay.type = "counts"
)

# Transform count assay to relative abundances
tse <- transformAssay(
  tse, assay.type = "counts",
  method = "relabundance"
)

# Define the variable of interest
group_var <- "diet"
```

### **ANCOM-BC II**

```{r analysis}
# Perform ANCOMBC-II
daa_results <- ancombc2(
  data = assay(tse, "relabundance"),
  meta_data = colData(tse),
  group = group_var,
  fix_formula = "diet",
  p_adj_method = "fdr",
  prv_cut = 0.05,
  lib_cut = 0,
  struc_zero = TRUE,
  neg_lb = TRUE,
  alpha = 0.05,
  global = TRUE
)

# Extract significant features
significant_results <- daa_results$res %>%
  filter(q_diet2 < 0.05) %>%  # Filter based on adjusted p-values
  arrange(q_diet2)  # Sort by adjusted p-value
```

### Results table

```{r table}
# Display results in a table
kable(
  significant_results,
  caption = "Differentially abundant features",
  col.names = c(
    "Taxon", "Intercept Effect Size", "Diet2 Effect Size", 
    "Intercept SE", "Diet2 SE", "Intercept W", "Diet2 W",
    "Intercept P-value", "Diet2 P-value", "Intercept Adjusted P-value",
    "Diet2 Adjusted P-value", "Intercept Significant", "Diet2 Significant",
    "Intercept Passed SS", "Diet2 Passed SS"
  )
)

filtered_results <- significant_results %>%
  select(taxon, lfc_diet2, q_diet2)

# Display results in a table
kable(
  filtered_results,
  caption = "Differentially abundant features for Diet2",
  col.names = c("Taxon", "Log Fold Change (Diet2)", "Adjusted P-value (Diet2)"))
```

### **Visualization**

#### Volcano plot

```{r volcano_plot}
# Volcano plot
ggplot(filtered_results, aes(x = lfc_diet2, y = -log10(q_diet2), color = taxon)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +  # Transparency for better visibility of overlapping points
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +  # Significance threshold
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +  # Zero log2 fold change line
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "fold-change (Diet2)",
    y = "-log10 (Adjusted P-value)"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1)  # Add black boundary
  ) +
  # Add labels for significant taxa (those with significant p-values and notable fold change)
  geom_text_repel(
    data = filtered_results %>% filter(q_diet2 < 0.05 & abs(lfc_diet2) > 0.5), # Customize thresholds
    aes(label = taxon),  # Replace `taxon` with the correct column for taxa names
    size = 3,  # Font size for labels
    max.overlaps = 10,
    box.padding = 0.3,
    point.padding = 0.2,
    direction = "both",
    nudge_y = 0.1
  )
```

####
